content.content:
# Erdős Problem #919

**Problem Number:** 919

**Status:** OPEN

**Problem Statement:**
Is there a graph G with vertex set ω₂² and chromatic number ℵ₂ such that every subgraph whose vertices have a lesser type has chromatic number ≤ ℵ₀?

What if instead we ask for G to have chromatic number ℵ₁?

**Tags:** graph theory, chromatic number

**Citation:** [Er69b]

**Context & History:**
The problem builds on Babai's theorem regarding graphs on well-ordered sets. Erdős and Hajnal demonstrated that this theorem doesn't generalize to higher cardinals. They constructed a graph on ω₁² with chromatic number ℵ₁ where every strictly smaller subgraph has chromatic number ≤ ℵ₀. The construction uses vertex pairs ordered lexicographically with edges connecting pairs (x_α₁, y_β₁) and (x_α₂, y_β₂) when α₁ < α₂ and β₁ < β₂.

A similar construction yields a graph on ω₂² with chromatic number ℵ₂ where every smaller subgraph has chromatic number ≤ ℵ₁.

**Additional Notes:**
- No formalized statement exists
- Discussion thread: 0 comments
- One user (Alfaiz) has marked interest

Here is all the content found on the page at [https://www.erdosproblems.com/919](https://www.erdosproblems.com/919):

---

**Problem Number:** 919

**Status:** OPEN

**Tags:** graph theory, chromatic number

**Problem Statement:**
Is there a graph G with vertex set omega\_2^2 and chromatic number aleph\_2 such that every subgraph whose vertices have a lesser type has chromatic number at most aleph\_0?

What if instead we ask for G to have chromatic number aleph\_1?

**Citation:** [Er69b]

**Context and History:**
The problem builds on Babai's theorem regarding graphs on well-ordered sets. Erdos and Hajnal demonstrated that this theorem does not generalize to higher cardinals. They constructed a graph on omega\_1^2 with chromatic number aleph\_1 where every strictly smaller subgraph has chromatic number at most aleph\_0. The construction uses vertex pairs ordered lexicographically with edges connecting pairs (x\_{alpha\_1}, y\_{beta\_1}) and (x\_{alpha\_2}, y\_{beta\_2}) when alpha\_1 < alpha\_2 and beta\_1 < beta\_2.

A similar construction yields a graph on omega\_2^2 with chromatic number aleph\_2 where every smaller subgraph has chromatic number at most aleph\_1.

**Additional Notes:**
- No formalized statement exists
- Discussion thread: 0 comments
- One user (Alfaiz) has marked interest
